text: Attorney Sandeep Seth admitted using ChatGPT "as a shortcut" to find case law and submitted fabricated and misrepresented citations in a patent infringement matter without independent verification. The court found all five Lexos attorneys violated Rule 11(b). Seth was fined $5,000 and had his pro hac vice admission revoked; three co-counsel were fined $3,000, $3,000, and $1,000 respectively for failure to verify their co-counsel's work.
deployer: Sandeep Seth (drafting attorney); Kenneth P. Kula; Eric W. Joe; David R. Cooper (co-counsel)
canonical_source_conflicted: Non-existent Tenth and Federal Circuit patent cases; Fed. R. Civ. P. 11(b)
mitigation_gap: Lead drafter used ChatGPT as research shortcut and admitted not verifying citations; four co-counsel signed filings without checking a co-counsel's citations.
reliance_or_harm: $12,000 total Rule 11 sanctions across four attorneys; Seth's pro hac vice admission revoked and required to self-report to his bar.
